content.page_title: Best Weight Loss Clinics in Montreal, Quebec (2026)
content.meta_description: Find top-rated weight loss clinics in Montreal, QC. Compare services, costs, and insurance coverage to start your journey today.
content.h1: Best Weight Loss Clinics in Montreal, Quebec
content.intro: Looking for professional weight loss support in Montreal? Whether you're seeking medical supervision, nutrition counselling, or structured programs, this guide highlights the most trusted weight loss clinics across Quebec's largest city. We've researched local options to help you find the right fit for your goals and budget.
content.local_context: Montreal residents have access to diverse weight loss services through both private clinics and publicly funded healthcare. Quebec's health system covers some medically necessary weight loss interventions through RAMQ, though many specialized clinics operate privately. The city has seen growing dema

top_picks.name: Wharton Medical Clinic Montreal
top_picks.description: Established medical weight loss clinic offering physician-supervised programs, appetite suppressant medications, and personalized nutrition planning. Locations across Montreal with experienced staff trained in bariatric medicine.
top_picks.best_for: People seeking medical oversight and those with metabolic concerns or comorbidities

top_picks.name: Ideal Protein Montreal Centre
top_picks.tagline: Structured meal replacement program with local support
top_picks.description: Popular ketogenic-based meal replacement system with weekly in-person coaching. Multiple locations throughout Montreal including the Plateau, Griffintown, and suburban areas. Focuses on rapid initial weight loss with behavioral support.
top_picks.best_for: People who prefer structured meal plans and those needing community accountability

top_picks.name: Centre de Santé Métabolique Montréal
top_picks.tagline: Comprehensive metabolic and weight management clinic
top_picks.description: Multidisciplinary clinic combining endocrinology, nutrition, and psychology. Offers metabolic testing, hormonal assessments, and personalized treatment plans. Accepts referrals from family doctors and can coordinate with RAMQ for eligible patients.
pros: RAMQ coverage available for eligible patients
top_picks.best_for: People with metabolic disorders, hormonal imbalances, or those seeking comprehensive medical evaluation

top_picks.name: Herbal Magic Montreal
top_picks.tagline: Natural supplement-based program with personalized plans
top_picks.description: Natural health clinic offering herbal supplements, detox programs, and personalized nutrition plans. Emphasizes whole foods and natural approaches to weight loss. Multiple Montreal locations with certified nutritionists on staff.
cons: Limited scientific evidence for some claims
top_picks.best_for: People preferring natural approaches and those seeking supplement-based support

top_picks.name: Clinique Bariatrique et Métabolique du Québec
top_picks.description: Specialized clinic for bariatric surgery assessment and post-operative care. Also offers non-surgical weight management programs. Serves as a referral centre for complex cases and provides pre- and post-surgery nutritional support.
top_picks.price_range: Varies; surgery may be covered by RAMQ if criteria met
cons: Longer wait times for RAMQ-covered surgery
top_picks.best_for: People considering or undergoing bariatric surgery and those with significant weight loss needs

top_picks.name: Nutrition Plus Montreal
top_picks.tagline: Registered dietitian-led nutrition and lifestyle coaching
top_picks.description: Private nutrition clinic staffed by registered dietitian nutritionists. Offers personalized meal planning, food psychology coaching, and sustainable lifestyle change programs. Evidence-based approach without supplements or meal replacements.
top_picks.best_for: People seeking sustainable lifestyle change and those wanting to work with registered dietitians

what_to_look_for: Qualified healthcare providers (doctors, registered dietitians, or certified nutritionists) with credentials you can verify
Clear pricing structure with no hidden fees; ask about payment plans or package discounts
Insurance coverage options, including whether RAMQ or private insurance can reimburse any services
Approach alignment with your preferences (medical supervision vs. natural, structured programs vs. personalized plans)
Realistic expectations and evidence-based methods rather than extreme promises or fad diets
Support system including follow-up appointments, community, or accountability mechanisms
content.average_cost_context: Montreal weight loss clinics typically range from $80-$400 CAD per month depending on services. Meal replacement programs average $120-200/month, while medical clinics charge $200-350/month. Some RAMQ coverage is available for metabolic clinics with a doctor's referral.
content.insurance_notes: In Quebec, RAMQ may cover weight loss services if referred by a family doctor and deemed medically necessary (e.g., for metabolic or endocrine conditions). Private insurance rarely covers weight loss programs. Always confirm coverage with your insurer before starting a program.

faqs.question: Does RAMQ cover weight loss clinics in Montreal?
faqs.answer: RAMQ may cover medically supervised weight loss services if referred by your family doctor and if you have a qualifying metabolic or endocrine condition. Coverage is not automatic; eligibility depends on your specific situation. Contact your clinic to confirm.

faqs.question: What's the difference between a dietitian and a nutritionist in Quebec?
faqs.answer: In Quebec, 'dietitian' and 'registered dietitian nutritionist' (RDN) are protected titles requiring university training and certification. 'Nutritionist' is not a protected title and may not require formal credentials. Always ask about qualifications.

faqs.question: How long does it take to see results at Montreal weight loss clinics?
faqs.answer: Most people see initial results (2-5 lbs) within 2-4 weeks with structured programs. Sustainable weight loss typically averages 1-2 lbs per week. Results vary based on individual metabolism, adherence, and the program type.

faqs.question: Can I get GLP-1 medications like Ozempic at Montreal weight loss clinics?
faqs.answer: Yes, some Montreal medical clinics prescribe GLP-1 medications for weight loss. However, these are prescription drugs requiring a doctor's assessment. Cost is typically $200-400/month and is rarely covered by insurance for weight loss (only for diabetes).

faqs.question: Are virtual weight loss consultations available in Montreal?
faqs.answer: Yes, many Montreal clinics now offer virtual consultations via phone or video. This can be convenient and is sometimes cheaper than in-person visits. Ask about virtual options when calling clinics.

faqs.question: What should I bring to my first weight loss clinic appointment?
faqs.answer: Bring your health card, a list of current medications, any recent blood work results, and your health history. Be prepared to discuss your weight loss goals, eating habits, exercise routine, and any previous weight loss attempts.

faqs.question: Do Montreal weight loss clinics offer maintenance programs after I reach my goal?
faqs.answer: Most reputable clinics offer maintenance support to help prevent weight regain. This may include periodic check-ins, ongoing coaching, or community programs. Ask about long-term support before enrolling.

content.cta_heading: Ready to Start Your Weight Loss Journey?
content.cta_body: Don't navigate this alone. Sign up for our Montreal Weight Loss Guide newsletter to receive updates on new clinics, expert tips, and exclusive local resources. Get started today and find the right clinic for your goals.
